name: MCP Toolbox for Databases (Google)
what_it_does: Official Google MCP Toolbox for Databases enabling AI agents to interact with Google Cloud databases — querying Cloud SQL, Spanner, AlloyDB, and BigQuery; managing database connections with connection pooling; and integrating Google Cloud's managed database services into agent-driven data workflows with built-in security and performance optimizations.
use_cases: Querying Cloud SQL (PostgreSQL, MySQL) from data retrieval agents
Running Spanner SQL queries for global distributed data from analytics agents
Querying BigQuery datasets for large-scale analytics from data science agents
Managing AlloyDB databases for high-performance PostgreSQL from platform agents
Building RAG pipelines that query structured Google Cloud data from AI agents
Connecting agents to Google Cloud databases with connection pooling for efficiency
not_for: Teams using AWS RDS, Azure SQL, or other non-GCP databases
Self-hosted database users without Google Cloud
Non-SQL workloads (Firestore, Bigtable have separate tools)
best_when: An agent needs to query or manage Google Cloud databases (Cloud SQL, Spanner, AlloyDB, BigQuery) with enterprise-grade connection management.
avoid_when: You're using AWS or Azure databases — or if you don't use Google Cloud managed databases.

auth.notes: Google Cloud service account or application default credentials (ADC). IAM controls database access. Service accounts with minimal IAM roles recommended for agents.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: Cloud SQL and BigQuery have free tier credits; toolbox itself is free
pricing.paid_tiers: Google Cloud database pricing varies by service and usage
pricing.notes: The MCP Toolbox is free and open source. Costs are for underlying Google Cloud database services (Cloud SQL, Spanner, BigQuery). GCP pricing varies by service.

performance.rate_limits: Database service rate limits apply; toolbox adds connection pooling

known_agent_gotchas: Requires Google Cloud project and database already provisioned — toolbox is a query layer
Service account credentials setup required — ADC simplifies local development
Each database type (Cloud SQL, Spanner, BigQuery) has slightly different SQL dialect
Cloud SQL requires Cloud SQL Auth Proxy or private connectivity for secure connections
Connection pooling configuration affects concurrency — tune max_pool_size for agents
BigQuery queries consume slots and can be costly — agents should use preview limits
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. IAM service account with granular roles. SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, GDPR, FedRAMP. Official Google APIs MCP toolbox. Use minimal-permission service accounts.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. IAM service accounts with minimal-privilege roles. SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, GDPR, FedRAMP. Official Google APIs toolbox.
